title: Sprint-aware recall from research_briefs on write cycles
problem: During BROWSE cycles the recall query is built from topic_summary.txt top keywords -- what is trending in the feed right now. When the active sprint task is [write] or [compile], this is the wrong signal: Sebastian enters the write cycle with memory primed for live feed topics rather than accumulated thinking on the sprint subject. research_briefs.json already contains rich, specific vocabulary for the sprint (brief, open_questions, belief_axes, compulsion) written during the ponder phase, but it is never read after creation. The result is that [write] tasks operate without access to the prior research the ponder phase explicitly synthesised.
evidence: state/sprint_context.txt shows the core [write] Veritas Lens v0.1 Specification task has been carried forward across multiple weeks -- the write cycle cannot draw on prior synthesis

state/research_briefs.json contains full Veritas Lens brief including open_questions (how will sources be justified to the public?), belief_axes (Integrity of Information, Truth and Evidence), methodology and risk factors -- precision vocabulary ideal for recall queries
runner/lib/pre_browse.js step 3 always derives recallQuery from topic_summary.txt top 3 keywords regardless of cycle intent -- no sprint-awareness
runner/write_article.js already uses db.recallMemory(topicKeywords, 10) for Moltbook articles, proving the pattern works -- it just needs to be applied to sprint write cycles too
browse_archive.md has 189 SPRINT-tagged entries and the memory index has indexed articles, checkpoints and journals -- the data is there, the query routing is wrong
proposed_solution: In runner/lib/pre_browse.js, augment step 3 (recall query derivation) to check if the active sprint task from sprint_context.txt is of type [write] or [compile]. If so, load research_briefs.json, find the plan whose title matches the sprint plan label, extract keywords from brief + open_questions + belief_axes fields using extractKeywords() from scraper/analytics (already available in the codebase), and use those as the recall query instead of topic_summary keywords. Fallback to topic_summary if no matching brief is found. No new files, no new pipeline steps. Changes: (1) runner/lib/pre_browse.js -- augment step 3 with sprint-brief recall path. (2) runner/lib/config.js -- add RESEARCH_BRIEFS_PATH constant (state/research_briefs.json).
resolution: Applied sprint-aware recall routing in runner/lib/pre_browse.js with research_briefs.json fallback for top pending write/compile sprint work, and added RESEARCH_BRIEFS_PATH to runner/lib/config.js.
